title: Functions demo
text: article demonstrate wrapper function.
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins)  theme_set(light_mode_rt())

title: Functions demo
code: economics |>   gg_area(     x = date,     y = unemploy,     y_title = "Unemployment",    )

title: Functions demo
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_bar(     y = species,     col = sex,     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = 0.75,   )

title: Functions demo
code: diamonds |>   gg_bin_2d(     x = carat,     y = price,   )

title: Functions demo
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_boxplot(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = sex,     col = species,     theme = light_mode_b(),   )

title: Functions demo
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    group_by(sex, species) |>   summarise(across(flipper_length_mm, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))) |>   gg_col(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = species,     col = sex,     position = position_dodge(preserve = "single"),     width = 0.75,   )

title: Functions demo
code: faithfuld |>  gg_contour(    x = waiting,    y = eruptions,    z = density,    )

title: Go further
text: article demonstrate advanced content, including : Use different fonts Visualise spatial data Change stat Use non-supported aesthetics Use delayed evaluation Add legend layers set single value Add contrasting dark light text polygons
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins)

title: Go further
text: showtext sysfonts packages support use different fonts Google. *_mode theme functions provide base_family, title_family, subtitle_family caption_family arguments. Fonts can identified Google fonts website.
code: sysfonts::font_add_google("Covered By Your Grace", "grace") sysfonts::font_add_google('Roboto Slab', 'roboto_slab') sysfonts::font_add_google('Syne Mono', 'syne')  showtext::showtext_auto(enable = TRUE)  penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     facet = species,     title = "palmerpenguins::penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",     theme = light_mode_rt(       base_size = 11,       base_family = "grace",        title_family = "roboto_slab",        subtitle_family = "syne"),     ) showtext::showtext_auto(enable = FALSE)

title: Go further
text: ggblanket wraps ggplot2::geom_sf ggplot2::geom_raster functions, spatial vector array data can visualised.
code: sf::st_read(system.file("shape/nc.shp", package = "sf"), quiet = TRUE) |>   gg_sf(col = AREA) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_axes() stars::read_stars(system.file("tif/L7_ETMs.tif", package = "stars")) |>  tibble::as_tibble() |>  gg_raster(    x = x,    y = y,    col = L7_ETMs.tif,    facet = band,    col_title = "L7 ETMs",   ) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_axes()

title: Go further
text: default stat gg_* function can changed.
code: penguins |>    gg_point(     stat = "sum",     x = species,     y = island,   ) +   labs(size = "Count") penguins |>   gg_pointrange(     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,      stat = "summary",      na.rm = TRUE,     size = 0.1,     x_labels = \(x) str_sub(x, 1, 1),   ) library(ggforce)  ggplot2::economics |>   slice_head(n = 35) |>    gg_path(     x = date,      y = unemploy,     stat = "bspline",     n = 100,      linewidth = 1,   )

title: Go further
text: functions commonly needed aesthetics directly available argument. available, can either use mapping argument. mapping argument provides access size, shape, linetype linewidth aesthetics within gg_* function.
code: penguins |>    gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,      y = body_mass_g,      col = species,     mapping = aes(shape = species),   ) +   labs(shape = "Species")

title: Go further
text: x, y, col alpha aesthetic arguments require unquoted variable, apply function . However, can aesthetics within mapping argument. useful delayed evaluation ggplot2::after_stat function.
code: penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     mappingRp = aes(y = after_stat(density)),     facet = species,   ) faithfuld |>   gg_contour(     x = waiting,     y = eruptions,     z = density,     mapping = aes(colour = after_stat(level)),     bins = 8,   )

what: 6. Add a legend from layers each set to a single value
title: Go further
text: add legend layers set single value, can likewise use mapping argument.
code: mpg |>    gg_point(     x = displ,      y = hwy,     mapping = aes(colour = "Point"),      col_pal = c(pal_navy, pal_teal),     col_title = "Legend",   ) +   geom_smooth(mapping = aes(colour = "Line"))

what: 7. Add contrasting dark or light text on polygons
title: Go further
text: method developed @teunbrand colour text polygons using dark light colour, determined based underlying polygon colour.
code: contrast <- function(col_pal,                       col_pal_dark = pal_light_mode["text"],                       col_pal_light = pal_light_mode["panel"]) {        out <- rep(col_pal_dark, length(col_pal))     light <- farver::get_channel(col_pal, "l", space = "hcl")     out[light < 50] <- col_pal_light     out   }  aes_contrast <- aes(colour = after_scale(contrast(fill)))  penguins |>   count(species, sex) |>    gg_col(     x = sex,     y = n,     col = species,     col_pal = c("#0095A8", "#112E51", "#FF7043"),     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     width = 0.75,      x_labels = \(x) str_to_sentence(x),   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(y = n - (max(n * 0.04)), label = n, !!!aes_contrast),     position = position_dodge2(width = 0.75, preserve = "single"),     size = 3.53,     show.legend = FALSE,   ) mtcars |>    corrr::correlate() |>    corrr::stretch() |>    mutate(across(c(x, y), str_to_sentence)) |>    gg_tile(     x = x,      y = y,     col = r,     x_title = "",      y_title = "",      x_expand = c(0, 0),      y_expand = c(0, 0),     col_pal_na = pal_light_mode["plot"],     col_limits = c(-1, 1),     col_rescale = scales::rescale(c(-1, 0, 1)),     col_title = "r",   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(label = round(r, 1), !!!aes_contrast),     size = 3.53,     show.legend = FALSE,   )

title: Extensions
text: article demonstrate fantastic ggplot2 extension packages.
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(palmerpenguins)

title: Extensions
text: patchwork package enables plots patched together. convenient hack add centred title non-faceted plot add facet_wrap layer character string facets argument.
code: library(patchwork)  p1 <- mtcars |>    gg_point(     x = mpg,      y = disp,     y_labels = \(x) replace_seq(x),   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 1")  p2 <- mtcars |>    gg_boxplot(     x = gear,      y = disp,      group = gear,      width = 0.5,     y_labels = \(x) replace_seq(x),   ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 2")  p3 <- mtcars |>    gg_smooth(     x = disp,      y = qsec,      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(4),    ) +   facet_wrap(~"Plot 3")   (p1 + p2) / p3 +   plot_annotation(     title = "A collection of plots patched together",     subtitle = "Works so nice!",     theme = light_mode_rt() +       theme(         plot.title = element_text(           face = "bold",           margin = margin(t = 10 * -0.5, b = 10 * 0.75)         ),         plot.subtitle = element_text(margin = margin()),       )   )

title: Extensions
text: ggalluvial package supports sankey charts.
code: library(ggalluvial)  titanic_wide <- data.frame(Titanic)    titanic_wide |>    gg_blanket(     geom = "alluvium",     stat = "alluvium",     y = Freq,      col = Survived,     mapping = aes(axis1 = Class, axis2 = Sex, axis3 = Age),     flipped = FALSE,     x_breaks = c(1, 2, 3),     x_labels = c("Class", "Sex", "Age"),     y_limits = c(NA, NA),     col_pal = c(pal_orange, pal_teal),   ) +   geom_stratum(     colour = "black",     fill = pal_light_mode["panel"],     show.legend = FALSE,   ) +   geom_text(     mapping = aes(label = after_stat(stratum)),     stat = "stratum",     colour = "black",     show.legend = FALSE,    ) +   guides(colour = "none") +   ggeasy::easy_remove_y_axis()

title: Extensions
text: ggblend package provides blending colours. Note must use graphics device supports blending.
code: penguins |>   gg_blanket(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     stat = "density",     col_pal = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(3, "Set1"),   ) +   geom_density(     alpha = 0.5,    ) |>    ggblend::blend(blend = "multiply")

title: Extensions
text: ggdensity package provides visualizations density estimates.
code: library(ggdensity)  data.frame(   x = rnorm(1000),   y = rnorm(1000),   z = c(rep("A", times = 500), rep("B", times = 500))   ) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "hdr",      stat = "hdr",     x = x,      y = y,      col = z,     facet = z,     colour = NA,   ) library(ggdensity)  data.frame(   x = rnorm(1000),   y = rnorm(1000),   z = c(rep("A", times = 500), rep("B", times = 500))   ) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "hdr",      stat = "hdr",     x = x,      y = y,      #this method of removing colour is better for the legend     col_pal = scales::alpha(pal_blue, 0),   )

title: Extensions
text: ggdist package enables visualisation uncertainty.
code: library(ggdist) library(distributional)  data.frame(   distribution = c("Gamma(2,1)", "Normal(5,1)", "Mixture"),   values = c(     dist_gamma(2, 1),     dist_normal(5, 1),     dist_mixture(       dist_gamma(2, 1),       dist_normal(5, 1),       weights = c(0.4, 0.6))   )) |>    gg_blanket(     geom = "slabinterval",     stat = "slabinterval",     y = distribution,     mapping = aes(dist = values),     y_expand = c(0.05, 0.05),     alpha_pal = 0.5,     # show_slab = FALSE,     # show_interval = FALSE,     # side = "both",   ) +   labs(width = "Width") library(ggdist)  tibble(x = 1:10) |>    group_by(across(everything()))  |>    do(tibble(y = rnorm(100, .$x))) |>    median_qi(.width = c(0.5, 0.8, 0.95)) |>   mutate(.width = factor(.width)) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "lineribbon",     x = x,     y = y,     ymin = .lower,     ymax = .upper,     col = .width,     colour = "black",     col_legend_rev = TRUE,     col_pal = rev(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(n = 3, "Blues")),   )

title: Extensions
text: ggeasy package provides lot support easily modifying themes. Removing axes often useful maps.
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col = sex,      col_title = "",     theme = light_mode_t(),   ) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_y_axis(what = c("line", "ticks"))

title: Extensions
text: ggforce package includes functions annotating areas points, geom making smoothed line graph using b-spline.
code: library(ggforce)  penguins |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "mark_hull",     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = species,     coord = coord_cartesian(),     alpha_pal = 0.5,   ) +   geom_point() economics |>   slice_head(n = 35) |>    gg_path(     stat = "bspline",     x = date,      y = unemploy,     n = 100,      linewidth = 1,   ) iris |>    mutate(across(Species, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_blanket(     geom = "shape",     stat = "voronoi_tile",     x = Sepal.Length,      y = Sepal.Width,      col = Species,     group = 1,     colour = pal_light_mode["panel"],     col_pal = c(pal_teal, pal_orange, pal_navy),   )

title: Extensions
text: ggh4x package includes enhanced facet functionality.
code: iris |>    rename_with(\(x) snakecase::to_snake_case(x)) |>    mutate(across(species, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    mutate(size = if_else(species == "Setosa", "Short Leaves", "Long Leaves")) |>    gg_point(     x = sepal_width,      y = sepal_length,      x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(n = 3),   ) +   ggh4x::facet_nested(     cols = vars(size, species),      nest_line = TRUE,      solo_line = TRUE,   ) +   theme(strip.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 2.5, b = 5)))

title: Extensions
text: gghighlight package enables geoms parts thereof highlighted.  gg_* function builds scale data thinks within panel. Therefore situations, may need make adjustments, scale builds correctly.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,   ) +   gghighlight::gghighlight(body_mass_g >= 5000) penguins |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = species,     x_breaks = scales::breaks_pretty(4),     col_pal = rep(pal_blue, 3),    ) + #build the scale for not faceted (i.e. all data)   facet_wrap(~species) + #then add facet layer   gghighlight::gghighlight()

title: Extensions
text: ggnewscale package enables multiple colour scales.
code: penguins |>    gg_blanket(     x = flipper_length_mm,      y = body_mass_g,   ) +    geom_point(     mapping = aes(colour = bill_length_mm),      data = penguins |> filter(species == "Gentoo"),   ) +   scale_color_viridis_c(option = "G", direction = -1) +   labs(colour = "Gentoo\nBill length (mm)") +   ggnewscale::new_scale_colour() +   geom_point(     mapping = aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g, colour = species),      data = penguins |> filter(species != "Gentoo"),   ) +   scale_color_manual(values = c(pal_plum, pal_orange)) +   labs(colour = "Species")

title: Extensions
text: ggrepel package can used neatly avoid overlapping labels.
code: mtcars |>   tibble::rownames_to_column("car") |>   filter(wt > 2.75, wt < 3.45) |>   gg_point(     x = wt,      y = mpg,      label = car,    ) +   ggrepel::geom_text_repel(     colour = pal_blue,      size = 3.53,   )

title: Extensions
text: ggridges package enables ridgeline plots.
code: library(ggridges)  ggridges::Catalan_elections |>   rename_with(snakecase::to_snake_case) |>   mutate(year = factor(year)) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "density_ridges",     stat = "density_ridges",     coord = coord_cartesian(),     x = percent,     y = year,     col = option,     colour = "white",     x_limits = c(0, 100),     y_expand = expansion(c(0, 0.125)),     alpha_pal = 0.7,   )

title: Extensions
text: ggtext package enables plot text use markdown syntax. applicable markdown theme elements need added plot. Note want title bold, ’s important change default theme plain title - otherwise gg_theme defaults bold.
code: penguins |> gg_point( x = bill_depth_mm, y = bill_length_mm, col = species, title = "***Pygoscelis*** **penguin** bill lengths and depths", subtitle = "**Adelie**, **Chinstrap**, *and* **Gentoo**", ) + theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown()) + theme(plot.subtitle = ggtext::element_markdown()) + guides(colour = "none", fill = "none") penguins |> gg_point( x = bill_depth_mm, y = bill_length_mm, col = species, facet = island, facet2 = sex, x_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), x_title = "**Bill depth** (mm)", y_labels = \(x) replace_seq(glue::glue("_{x}_")), y_title = "**Bill length** (mm)", col_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), col_title = "**Species**", facet_labels = \(x) glue::glue("_{x}_"), title = "***Pygoscelis*** **penguin** bill lengths and depths", subtitle = "**Adelie**, **Chinstrap**, *and* **Gentoo**", ) + theme( plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(), plot.subtitle = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.title.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.title.y = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), axis.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown(), legend.title = ggtext::element_markdown(), legend.text = ggtext::element_markdown(), strip.text.x = ggtext::element_markdown(), strip.text.y = ggtext::element_markdown()) + guides(colour = "none", fill = "none")

title: Extensions
text: geomtextpath package enables curved text.
code: library(geomtextpath)  expand.grid(x = 1:nrow(volcano), y = 1:ncol(volcano)) |>    mutate(z = c(volcano)) |>    gg_contour_filled(     x = x,      y = y,     z = z,     binwidth = 20,     x_expand = c(0, 0),     x_limits = c(NA, NA),     y_limits = c(NA, NA)) +   geom_textcontour(     aes(label = after_stat(level)),      size = 2.5,      straight = TRUE,   ) +   ggeasy::easy_remove_axes()

title: Extensions
text: ggiraph package enables interactive plots.
code: p <- diamonds |>   gg_blanket(     x = color,     y_expand_limits = 0,     stat = "count",      theme = light_mode_rt(       base_family = "arial",        base_size = 9     ),   ) +   ggiraph::geom_bar_interactive(     mapping = aes(tooltip = after_stat(count), data_id = color),      width = 0.75,      colour = pal_blue,      fill = pal_blue,      alpha = 0.9   )  ggiraph::girafe(   ggobj = p,    height_svg = 3,    width_svg = 5,   options = list(     ggiraph::opts_sizing(rescale = TRUE, width = 0.7),     ggiraph::opts_tooltip(use_fill = TRUE),      ggiraph::opts_hover(css = "stroke:black;stroke-width:1px;"))   )

title: Extensions
text: plotly::ggplotly function enables interactive plots. Using text aesthetic provides flexibility tooltips. Alternatively, can use plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = c("x", "y", "fill")) plotly::ggplotly(p) quick tooltips.
code: p <- diamonds |>   group_by(color) |>   count(name = "count") |>   mutate(text = glue::glue("Colour: {color}                             Count: {count}")) |>   gg_col(     x = color,     y = count,     text = text,     width = 0.75,     y_expand_limits = 0,     theme = light_mode_rt(       base_family = "arial"     ),   )  plotly::ggplotly(p, tooltip = "text")

title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket package ggplot2 wrapper functions. primary objective simplify ggplot2 visualisation. Secondary objectives relate : Design: produce well-designed visualisation default Alignment: use conventions generally aligned ggplot2 Scope: cover majority ggplot2 .

title: ggblanket
text: Almost forty gg_* wrapper functions col argument colour fill variable facet argument facet variable facet2 argument facet 2nd variable col_pal argument customise colours Prefixed arguments customise Unspecified titles snakecase::sentence_case light_mode_* dark_mode_* theme families Access geom_* arguments via ... Symmetric continuous positional scales gridlines removal Flexibility turn-fix either colour fill Ability add multiple geom_* layers key differences ggplot2 gg_blanket() function geom flexibility
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(stringr) library(tidyr) library(palmerpenguins)

title: ggblanket
text: gg_* function wraps ggplot2 ggplot(aes(...)) function applicable ggplot2 geom_*() function. gg_* function named geom_* function wrap.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,   )

what: 2. A single col argument to colour and fill by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: colour fill aesthetics ggplot2 merged single concept represented col argument. combined aesthetic means everything coloured according , .e. points, lines polygon interiors. flexibility undo combined aesthetic necessary, discussed later article.
code: penguins |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,      col = species,   ) penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_bar(     y = species,      col = sex,     width = 0.75,     position = "dodge",   )

what: 3. A facet argument to facet by a variable
title: ggblanket
text: Faceting treated aesthetic. Users just provide unquoted variable facet . Whenever facet variable provided (facet2 variable provided), facet_layout default "wrap".
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = species,   )

what: 4. A facet2 argument to facet by a 2nd variable
title: ggblanket
text: facet2 argument also provided extra functionality flexibility. enables users facet easily "grid" layout facet variable horizontally facet2 variable vertically. Whenever facet2 variable provided, facet_layout default "grid".
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     facet = species,     facet2 = sex,   )

what: 5. A col_pal argument to customise colours
title: ggblanket
text: col_pal argument used customise colours geom. user can provide vector colours argument. can named . works consistent way - regardless whether col aesthetic added .
code: penguins |>   gg_jitter(     x = species,      y = body_mass_g,      col_pal = "#112E51",   ) penguins |>    drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_density(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     col_pal = c("#112E51", "#78909C"),   )

title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket offers numerous arguments customise prefixed whether relate x, y, col, facet alpha. prefixed arguments work nicely Rstudio autocomplete, users ensure settings support use tab autocompletions multi-line autocompletions (.e. Tools - Global Options - Code - Completion)
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>     gg_jitter(     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = flipper_length_mm,     facet = sex,     x_labels = \(x) str_sub(x, 1, 1),     y_expand_limits = 0,     y_breaks = scales::breaks_width(1500),      y_labels = scales::label_number(big.mark = " "),      y_expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05)),     y_transform = "sqrt",     y_title = "Body mass (g)",     col_continuous_type = "steps",     facet_labels = \(x) str_to_sentence(x),   )

title: ggblanket
text: Unspecified x, y, col alpha titles converted sentence case snakecase::to_sentence. However, title can manually changed using applicable *_title argument. Use *_title = "" remove title. default snakecase::to_sentence conversion can turned using titles = \(x) x.
code: diamonds |>   gg_hex(     x = carat,     y = price,     coord = coord_cartesian(clip = "on"),      y_limits = c(0, 20000),   )

title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket provides two families themes: light_mode_* dark_mode_*. theme family provides 5 variants differ based legend placement, represented suffix theme name rt (right-top), r (right-centre), b (bottom), t (top) (inside panel). can set theme globally using ggplot2::theme_set().
code: # theme_set(dark_mode_rt())  penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     title = "Penguins body mass by flipper length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago, Antarctica",     caption = "Source: Gorman, 2020",      theme = dark_mode_b(title_face = "bold"),   )

what: 9. Access to other geom_* arguments via ...
title: ggblanket
text: ... argument provides access arguments geom_*() function.
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>    drop_na(sex) |>    gg_smooth(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,      se = TRUE, # via ... from geom_smooth     level = 0.99, # via ... from geom_smooth   )

what: 10. Symmetric continuous positional scales and gridlines removal
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket guesses whether plot flipped order determine: scale make symmetric default *_gridlines remove default flipped argument can specified get right. symmetric continuous y scale non-flipped plots defaults : y_limits range y_breaks y_expand c(0, 0) symmetric scale can turned easily using y_limits = c(NA, NA) (providing y_expand value). opposite occurs flipped plots x scale. Sometimes may need add *_expand_limits = 0, lower bound symmetric scale falls close low number. Note symmetric scale occur scale transformation "identity", "reverse", "date", "time" "hms" - positional scale binned. non-flipped plots, x_gridlines removed default - y_gridlines removed default flipped plots. can adjust needed using *_gridlines arguments. Subsequently, recommended themes gridlines x y directions used ggblanket.
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) stringr::str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,   ) penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(across(sex, \(x) stringr::str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_point(     x = flipper_length_mm,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,     y_limits = c(NA, NA),   )

what: 11. Flexibility to turn-off or fix either colour or fill
title: ggblanket
text: geom outlines fill, default colour default value alpha_pal. However, geoms, can turn either colour (.e. outlines) fill, fix one constant colour. recommended approach use colour = NA/colour = black, fill = NA/fill = "lightgrey", add col aesthetic don’t already one (well removing legend). alternative approach set alpha_pal = 0 col_pal = scales::alpha(..., alpha = 0) can used. However, approach flexible.
code: penguins |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_boxplot(     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     colour = "black", #or colour = NA, or fill = NA or fill = "lightgrey" etc     position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single"),     alpha_pal = 0.9,   ) penguins |>   mutate(hack = "") |>    gg_boxplot(     x = species,     y = flipper_length_mm,     col = hack,     colour = "black", #or colour = NA, or fill = NA or fill = "lightgrey" etc     width = 0.5,     col_pal = pal_blue,     alpha_pal = 0.9,   ) +   guides(colour = "none", fill = "none")

what: 12. Ability to add multiple geom_* layers
title: ggblanket
text: Users can make plots multiple layers ggblanket adding ggplot2::geom_* layers. gg_* function puts aesthetic variables (.e. x, y, col) within wrapped ggplot function. Therefore, aesthetics inherit subsequent layers added. facet facet2 likewise inherit - data, coord theme. gg_*() function appropriate bottom layer plot. geoms plot order. aesthetics required plot whole generally added gg_* function, including col argument. want first layer colour fill aesthetic, wish later layers access - use gg_blanket() (defaults blank geom). Use *_expand_limits *_limits ensure `gg_* function builds limits appropriately plot whole.
code: penguins |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>    mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.2) %>%   gg_col(     x = body_mass_g,     xmin = lower,      xmax = upper,     y = species,     col = species,     width = 0.75,     x_expand_limits = c(0, max(.$upper)),     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg",    ) +   geom_errorbar(     colour = "black",      width = 0.1,    ) penguins |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(body_mass_g = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   mutate(lower = body_mass_g * 0.95) |>    mutate(upper = body_mass_g * 1.2) |>    gg_blanket(      x = body_mass_g,     y = species,     col = species,     xmin = lower,      xmax = upper,     width = 0.75,     x_expand_limits = 0,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,      x_title = "Body mass kg",   ) +   geom_col(     colour = "#d3d3d3",#fix to constant     fill = "#d3d3d3",#fix to constant     alpha = 0.9,     width = 0.75,   ) +   geom_errorbar(     width = 0.1,    )

previous_headings: How it works > 13. Other ggplot2 key differences
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket requires unquoted variables x, y, col, facet, facet2 alpha. wrap function. Instead may need manipulate data prior plotting, use mapping argument non-supported aesthetics, delayed evaluation etc.
code: diamonds |>   count(color) |>   mutate(color = color |>            forcats::fct_reorder(n) |>            forcats::fct_rev()) |>   gg_col(     x = n,     y = color,     width = 0.75,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",    )

previous_headings: How it works > 13. Other ggplot2 key differences
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket keeps unused factor levels plot. users wish drop unused levels likewise data prior plotting using forcats::fct_drop.
code: diamonds |>    count(color) |>   filter(color %in% c("E", "G", "I")) |>   mutate(color = forcats::fct_drop(color)) |>    gg_point(     x = n,     y = color,     x_labels = \(x) x / 1000,     x_title = "Count (thousands)",    )

previous_headings: How it works > 13. Other ggplot2 key differences
title: ggblanket
text: default, ggblanket keeps values outside limits (*_oob = scales::oob_keep) calculating geoms scales plot. also clip anything outside cartesian coordinate space default (coord = ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "")). ggplot2 default drops values outside limits calculating geoms scales plot (scales::oob_censor), clips anything outside cartesian coordinate space (coord = ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "")). needs considered using x_limits y_limits, stat "identity".

previous_headings: How it works > 13. Other ggplot2 key differences
title: ggblanket
text: alpha fully supported ggblanket similar way col. alpha argument represents aesthetic, whereas alpha_pal represents value (values range) alpha alpha.

previous_headings: How it works > 13. Other ggplot2 key differences
title: ggblanket
text: *_title equivalent ggplot2::labs(* = ...) Facet arguments strip.position switch renamed facet_labels_position facet_labels_switch y categorical variables reversed, read low levels (top) high levels (bottom) * Logical variables reversed, TRUE always comes FALSE Infinite values treated NA

what: 14. A gg_blanket function with geom flexibility
title: ggblanket
text: ggblanket driven gg_blanket function, geom argument geom_blank defaults. functions wrap function locked-geom, default stat position arguments per applicable geom_* function.
code: penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "bar",     stat = "bin",     position = "stack",     x = flipper_length_mm,      col = sex,   ) penguins |>   drop_na(sex) |>   mutate(across(sex, \(x) str_to_sentence(x))) |>   gg_blanket(     geom = "violin",     stat = "ydensity",     position = "dodge",     x = species,     y = body_mass_g,     col = sex,   )

title: Simplify ggplot2 Visualisation
text: ggblanket package ggplot2 wrapper functions. primary objective simplify ggplot2 visualisation. Secondary objectives relate : Design: produce well-designed visualisation default Alignment: use conventions generally aligned ggplot2 Scope: cover majority ggplot2 .

title: Simplify ggplot2 Visualisation
code: install.packages("ggblanket")

title: Simplify ggplot2 Visualisation
code: library(ggblanket) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(palmerpenguins)  penguins |>   tidyr::drop_na(sex) |>    mutate(sex = stringr::str_to_sentence(sex)) |>   gg_histogram(     x = flipper_length_mm,     col = sex,     facet = species,     theme = light_mode_b(),   )

"free_x" "free_y"). "free_x" "free_y"). Defaults "fixed". title Title string. subtitle Subtitle string. caption Caption title string. titles function format unspecified titles. ggblanket 5.9.9000
text: Rewrote code completely. Exported gg_blanket function, allows ggproto geom (character string) added. function’s help inputs inherits gg_blanket (@olivroy, #625). Added stat support ggproto object (character string). Added position support ggproto object (character string). Added transform support transform class object (character string). Added alpha aesthetic support new alpha, alpha_pal alpha_* arguments. Added facet_axes facet_axis_labels arguments. Added facet_labels_position argument. Added light_mode_* dark_mode_* family functions. Added col_continuous_type argument support colouring “steps”. Added gg_quantile, gg_rug, gg_function functions. Added flipped argument. Changed *_mode_* default base_size 11. Changed *_mode_* default face plain. Changed *_mode_* base_pal 5 colour vector colour whole plot. Changed default theme place legend top right. Changed NULL effect pretty axis *_limits = c(NA, NA) y_expand NULL. Improved default gridlines. Fixed bug col logical class. Added new helper hex codes pal_blue, pal_teal, pal_orange, pal_navy, pal_plum pal_grey Updated default discrete palette. Breaking: shift ... front require users name arguments. Breaking: renamed pal col_pal. Breaking: renamed pal_na col_pal_na. Breaking: renamed alpha alpha_pal. Breaking: alpha now refers aesthetic . Breaking: renamed *_include *_expand_limits. Breaking: renamed *_trans *_transform. Breaking: removed col_legend_place argument. Breaking: removed light_mode dark_mode functions. Breaking: removed col_scale argument. Breaking: removed gg_blank function. Breaking: renamed facet_switch argument facet_labels_switch. Breaking: removed guardian function. Breaking: renamed str_keep_seq replace_seq. Improved documentation help (@olivroy, #643). Improved log defaults.

title: ggblanket 5.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-11-10 Updated colours: #357BA2 mako[9](5) col aesthetic, guardian discrete 4 less colours, scales:hue_pal 5 colours, viridisLite::mako reversed continuous, "grey" NA. Breaking: removed pal_* functions. Removed unnecessary messages. Fixed bug col_breaks working numeric col. Fixed bug str_keep_seq working date, datetime time class. Fixed bug time class variables work. Fixed bug gg_contour_filled gg_density_filled default number colours. Improved default positional breaks. Ensured positional breaks take expand account. Updated gg_qq, includes geom_qq_line layer, nicer x_title, y_title coord defaults. Added positional scale arguments gg_sf (.e. *_limits, *_expand, *_breaks, *_labels). Improved margins col_legend_place top.

title: ggblanket 5.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-19 Fixed bug col variable reversing non-flipped. Added pal_light_mode pal_dark_mode.

title: ggblanket 5.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-12 Added mapping argument allow extra aesthetics included, shape (use delayed evaluation aesthetics col, colour, fill alpha). Added stat argument back flexibility. Added linetype_title, shape_title, size_title arguments. Added str_keep_seq helper function support keeping labels sequence. Breaking: made horizontal y characters factors plot values low bottom high top. Breaking: removed lower, middle, upper, xlower, xmiddle, xupper arguments gg_blank. Updated *_breaks defaults. Made plot order logical variables TRUE first. Added pal_discrete2 6 colour blind safe colours light background theme.

title: ggblanket 4.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-08-08 Breaking: removed stat argument gg_* functions except gg_blank. Breaking: renamed pal_hue pal_discrete made colours relatively red/green colour blind safe. Breaking: renamed gg_bin2d gg_bin_2d. Breaking: changed *_oob default back scales::oob_keep. Improved scales work better transformations, limits, breaks etc. Added gg_contour, gg_contour_filled, gg_density2d, gg_density2d_filled functions. Made default keep unused factors across scales facets. Made default continuous label function drop trailing zeros. Made default legend place simpler: bottom, unless continuous. Otherwise right. Added text aesthetic plotly::ggplotly.

title: ggblanket 3.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-06-30 New theme functions: light_mode dark_mode. Breaking: removed gg_theme. Breaking: renamed *_grid arguments *_gridlines. Breaking: removed void argument. Breaking: removed gg_function. Breaking: made col_rescale require function (e.g. scales::rescale()). Added facet_switch argument. Made col_legend_place = "none" remove col legend. Made gg_bin2d gg_hex scales calculate way functions. raster, removed col aesthetic legend work. Improved gg_sf default alpha. Supported gg_sf work non-standard named geometry.

title: ggblanket 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-05-26 Made ggblanket work better adding layers. Made pal longer inherit layers col aesthetic. Made x y scales work better, including NA limits. Added 2 colours pal_hue. Breaking: changed default *oob argument scales::oob_censor. Breaking: changed default coord argument include clip = "" argument. Breaking: removed add_tooltip function. Breaking: changed pal functions pal_blue, pal_grey, pal_hue.

title: ggblanket 1.8.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-24 Updated default colour palette. Updated default NA colour.

title: ggblanket 1.7.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-03-06 Updated gg_theme default axis-line gridline thickness. Supported named pal vectors. Removed clip argument. Added coord argument. Added *_oob arguments. Improved *_limits works. Removed default gridlines gg_sf. Fixed bug default x_expand. Fixed bug gg_histogram scales. Critical fix support dplyr 1.1.0. Supported colouring date, datetime time variables. Made datetime breaks default waiver. Breaking: removed void argument gg_theme. Corrected bug gg_raster legend showing. Updated gg_raster *_limits *_expand defaults. Internal function make infinite values NA made compatible dplyr 1.1.0 (#269, @DavisVaughan)

title: ggblanket 1.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-11-18 Breaking: renamed add_tooltip_text add_tooltip changed column name “tooltip”. Breaking: coord argument removed. Added clip argument defaulted “”. Made x y limits also act within coord. Made gg_blank powerful flexible. Improved horizontal flipped plot scales. Fixed bugs relating free facet_scales scale limits. Improved vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-10-14 Breaking: redesigned gg_theme. Added automatic gridline removal. Added gg_bin2d function. Added gg_hex function. Added col_trans col_rescale arguments. Made col_legend_place default “r” numeric col variable. Made *_title = "" equivalent + labs(* = NULL). Supported x y datetime variables. Supported x y time variables. Redesigned internal code x, y col scales.

title: ggblanket 1.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-09-02 Breaking: Removed col_intervals argument. Breaking: Removed *_oob argument. Breaking: gg_theme, changed *_style arguments *_face. Breaking: gg_theme, changed font arguments family. Added gg_polygon function. Added col_continuous argument support colouring “steps”. Added col_legend_rev argument reverse legends. Added facet_layout argument faceting flexibility. Added facet_space argument support proportional facet panels. Removed width default. Improved default legend look continuous gradient. Improved scales y NULL plot horizontal. Fixed bug position = "fill". Updated messages. Added vignette.

title: ggblanket 1.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-08-05 Breaking: removed facet_intervals argument. Breaking: made extensive changes gg_theme function. Added facet2 aesthetic support effortless grid faceting. Changed default background theme colours. Fixed bug *_limits *_include working correctly. Added *_sec_axis arguments. Changed *_oob default scales::oob_keep. Removed reversal logical variable order. Defaulted col_legend_place "b".

title: ggblanket 1.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-07-01 Breaking: Removed *_breaks_n *_breaks_width. Added support *_breaks receive function. Added *_include argument. Added *_trans argument. Modified default x_breaks behaviour x y numeric date. Enhanced gg_blank work one x y. Provided support ggplotly. Added new add_tooltip_text function. Added new titles argument apply function across titles. Support gg_ functions working stats. Removed size argument, anticipation ggplot2 3.4.0. Updated gg_theme title vjust margins.

title: ggblanket 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-27 Remove default sentence case transformation categorical variable labels. Updated scales work consistent way position = "fill".

title: ggblanket 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 Initial release.
